349.reason: Error: When encountering a single '&', the code incorrectly adds an AND token with value "&" instead of reporting an error without adding a token. According to the problem description, a single '&' should be an error (type 'a'), and no token should be generated for it.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: When encountering a single '&', the code incorrectly adds an AND token with value "&" instead of reporting an error without adding a token. According to the problem description, a single '&' should be an error (type 'a'), and no token should be generated for it.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: When encountering a single '&', the code incorrectly adds an AND token with value "&" before adding the error. According to the specification, a single '&' should be treated as an error (type 'a') and should not produce a valid token. The token should not be added.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Incorrectly adds a token for invalid single '&' and does not return/stop execution

353.reason: [This line unconditionally adds "&&" token even when ch != '&', causing two tokens: one "&" and one "&&". It should be inside an else block or only added when ch == '&'.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: This line is executed unconditionally after the if block, meaning even when a single '&' is detected (and an error is added), it still adds an AND token with value "&&", which is incorrect. It should only add the token when the condition ch == '&' is true.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: This line is executed unconditionally after the if-else block, meaning that even when a single '&' is encountered (and an error is added), it still adds an AND token with value "&&". This is incorrect because the single '&' should not produce any token, and the "&&" token should only be added when two consecutive '&' are found.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This line unconditionally adds "&&" token even when ch != '&', causing two tokens: one "&" and one "&&". It should be inside an else block or only added when ch == '&'.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: This token is added unconditionally even when a single '&' is detected and an error is added. Should be inside an else block.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Unconditionally adds '&&' token even if the input was a single '&' (error case), resulting in duplicate/incorrect tokens
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This line incorrectly adds "&&" token even when only single '&' is present. It should only add "&&" when ch == '&', otherwise it should not add this token.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This line incorrectly adds "&&" token even when only a single '&' is present; it should only add "&&" when ch == '&', and in the error case, should not add any AND token beyond the single '&' already added]

357.reason: Error: Similar to the '&' case, when encountering a single '|', the code incorrectly adds an OR token with value "|" instead of just reporting the error without adding a token.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: Similar to the '&' case, when encountering a single '|', the code incorrectly adds an OR token with value "|" instead of just reporting the error without adding a token.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: Similar to the '&' case, when encountering a single '|', the code incorrectly adds an OR token with value "|" before adding the error. A single '|' should be an error and should not produce a valid token.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Incorrectly adds a token for invalid single '|' and does not return/stop execution

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: This line is executed unconditionally, so even when a single '|' is encountered (and an error is added), it still adds an OR token with value "||". This is incorrect; the "||" token should only be added when two consecutive '|' are found.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: unconditionally adds "||" token even for a single '|'.]

217.reason: The use of reader.ready() is unreliable for detecting EOF in PushbackReader/FileReader contexts; it may return false even when data is available, causing the lexer to terminate prematurely and produce empty output. It should read directly and check for -1.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The use of reader.ready() is unreliable for detecting EOF in PushbackReader/FileReader contexts; it may return false even when data is available, causing the lexer to terminate prematurely and produce empty output. It should read directly and check for -1.
